title: Fix stale hidden-tab activity decorations
description: Tab activity decorations can remain stale until the user visits the tab because inactive-tab terminal title changes are not always delivered through PaneUpdate. Fix the pane orchestrator so title-derived busy/stale markers converge from events and a scoped polling backup without requiring tab focus.
close_reason: Fixed hidden-tab activity decoration staleness by always subscribing to pane close/command exit events and adding scoped terminal-title polling for inactive tabs or existing terminal-title facts. Validation: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link; main runtime override build with ../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator.

title: Refactor tab identity and pane-cache state boundaries
description: Refactor the pane-orchestrator state added during the tab-id fix so durable tab identity and tab-local pane caches have explicit ownership boundaries instead of remaining as a wide set of parallel State fields. This is a behavior-preserving refactor: tab_id remains the owned state key, tab position remains only a Zellij/public-schema translation detail.
acceptance_criteria: Introduce typed state boundaries for tab identity and rebuilt tab-local pane caches; keep tab_id as the only owned tab-local state key while tab position remains only a Zellij/public-schema translation input/output; preserve active-tab session serialization and user-visible pane/layout behavior; do not change command surface or runtime contract; verify with cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo test --lib, cargo check --bin yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator, and nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link.
close_reason: Completed behavior-preserving refactor. Introduced TabIdentityState for active tab id/position and position/id maps, introduced TabPaneCaches for rebuilt tab-local pane caches, kept tab_id as owned state key and tab position as translation/public-schema data, and preserved command/runtime behavior. Verification passed: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo check --bin yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link.

title: Honor enabled status usage providers in refresh timers
description:
## Why

Yazelix can display only the Codex usage widget while the pane-orchestrator still wakes every agent-usage provider refresh loop. In the live slowdown investigation, the generated bar referenced only Codex usage, but the orchestrator heartbeat showed `claude_usage`, `codex_usage`, and `opencode_go_usage` refresh starts at the same timestamp. This adds unnecessary timer work and command-spawn pressure in sessions that do not render Claude or OpenCode usage.

## Evidence

- `src/main.rs:135`: plugin load schedules the Claude usage refresh unconditionally
- `src/main.rs:136`: plugin load schedules the Codex usage refresh unconditionally
- `src/main.rs:137`: plugin load schedules the OpenCode Go usage refresh unconditionally
- `src/main.rs:199`: every timer event handles the Claude usage refresh loop
- `src/main.rs:200`: every timer event handles the Codex usage refresh loop
- `src/main.rs:201`: every timer event handles the OpenCode Go usage refresh loop
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:138`: Claude usage timer has no enabled-provider guard
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:157`: Codex usage timer has no enabled-provider guard
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:176`: OpenCode Go usage timer has no enabled-provider guard
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:219`: Claude refresh records a heartbeat start and spawns `status-cache-refresh-claude-usage`
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:241`: Codex refresh records a heartbeat start and spawns `status-cache-refresh-codex-usage`
- `src/status_bar_cache.rs:263`: OpenCode Go refresh records a heartbeat start and spawns `status-cache-refresh-opencode-go-usage`

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a versioned pane-orchestrator configuration surface for enabled status usage providers, or equivalent child-owned contract
- Gate initial scheduling, timer handling, heartbeat refresh records, and helper command spawns to the enabled providers
- Keep Codex-only sessions from scheduling Claude/OpenCode usage refreshes
- Preserve behavior when all three providers are enabled

Out of scope:
- Main Yazelix generated KDL/materialization changes except as integration context
- zjstatus runtime bar template pruning
- Changing provider cache formats or tokenusage/OpenCode collection logic
- Local-only patches to `~/.local/share/yazelix` generated state

## Implementation Notes

1. Define an explicit provider selection model with stable identifiers: `claude_usage`, `codex_usage`, and `opencode_go_usage`.
2. Parse provider selection from plugin configuration or from a versioned runtime-config payload owned by the integration layer.
3. Do not schedule disabled providers at load time.
4. Make timer handlers no-op without re-arming disabled providers.
5. Ensure heartbeat status refreshes only list providers whose refresh loop is active or clearly mark disabled providers if the heartbeat contract needs that visibility.
6. Keep the default conservative for standalone use; if no provider selection is supplied, preserve current all-provider behavior unless the main integration supplies a narrower default.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` should exit 0
- `cargo test --lib` should exit 0 and include coverage for Codex-only provider selection
- `cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release` should exit 0
- `nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij plugin configuration cannot deliver provider selection without a main-repo contract change; leave the child API prepared and document the required main integration
- Stop if disabling providers would make existing standalone layouts lose expected usage widgets by default
- Stop if the heartbeat contract needs a schema change that main runtime code cannot consume safely yet

## Maintenance Notes

- Dependency decision: use existing Rust std/serde plumbing only; no new production or dev crates expected
- Main Yazelix owns the integrated widget tray and release transaction; this child bead owns the plugin source behavior only
notes:
Implemented provider-selection config in the pane orchestrator. The plugin reads status_usage_provider_claude_enabled, status_usage_provider_codex_enabled, and status_usage_provider_opencode_go_enabled from plugin configuration; missing keys preserve standalone all-provider behavior. Initial scheduling and timer handlers no-op disabled providers so disabled Claude/OpenCode/Codex refresh loops do not record heartbeat refresh starts or spawn helper commands.

Verification passed locally: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo test --lib; cargo build --target wasm32-wasip1 --profile release; nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link --no-write-lock-file.

Local integration evidence: Home Manager generation 604 was activated from local main Yazelix and pane-orchestrator overrides with --no-write-lock-file. Generated Zellij config contains Codex-only provider flags: claude=false, codex=true, opencode_go=false.

Remaining before close: publish this child commit, update the main flake.lock to the published revision, run main no-overrides validation, and manually validate a fresh Yazelix session heartbeat. Keep open because local runtime override is useful dogfooding evidence but not a durable release transaction.

title: Preserve wasm Rust toolchain after preBuild
description: Child implementation for main Yazelix issue #625. The package buildPhase must select the wasm-capable Fenix Cargo/Rustc/PATH before runHook preBuild, preserve that selected environment across preBuild, export CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, and serialize the wasm cargo build so Darwin hooks cannot leave Cargo resolving a Rust toolchain without wasm32-wasip1 std/core.
acceptance_criteria: Done when the package selects Fenix CARGO/RUSTC/PATH before preBuild, restores that environment after preBuild, exports CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, uses the wasm-capable toolchain for cargo build, serializes the wasm build, and nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link passes.
notes: Main public issue: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/625. Main release bead: yazelix-fhfdu.
close_reason: Implemented preserve-across-preBuild wasm toolchain handling. buildPhase now sets Fenix CARGO/RUSTC/PATH before runHook preBuild, saves those values, restores them after preBuild, exports CARGO_BUILD_RUSTC, checks the wasm32-wasip1 target libdir, and runs serialized cargo build. Metadata now declares wasmToolchainPreservedAcrossPreBuild. Verified nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link --no-write-lock-file.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Reopened after Tyce confirmed the previous post-preBuild reconstruction still failed on Darwin, specifically in pane-orchestrator; fix needs to preserve the selected wasm toolchain across preBuild.
